packages.name: express
packages.synopsis: Package express is a fast, minimalist web framework for Go.
packages.doc:
Package express is a fast, minimalist web framework for Go.

It provides routing, middleware, and a familiar handler API modelled on the
Node.js Express framework.

Basic usage

Create a router, register handlers, and listen:

	r := express.New()
	r.Get("/", func(c *express.Context) { c.Send("hello") })
	r.Listen(":3000")

See https://expressjs.com for the original API this mirrors.

consts.doc: Version is the semantic version of the express module.

vars.signature: var ErrNotFound = errors.New("express: route not found")
vars.doc: ErrNotFound is returned when no route matches the request.

types.name: Router
types.doc:
Router dispatches incoming requests to registered handlers. A Router is
safe for concurrent use once handlers are registered.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc: New allocates and returns an empty Router.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc: Get registers h to handle GET requests for the given path pattern.

methods.name: Listen
methods.doc:
Listen binds addr and serves requests until the process exits.
It blocks and only returns on a fatal error.

examples.code:
r := express.New()
r.Get("/", func(c *express.Context) { c.Send("hi") })
r.Listen(":3000")

types.name: HandlerFunc
types.doc: HandlerFunc is the signature every route handler must satisfy.

funcs.name: Static
funcs.doc: Static returns a handler that serves files from dir on disk.

packages.name: middleware
packages.synopsis: Package middleware provides reusable request middleware.
packages.doc:
Package middleware provides reusable request middleware for the express
router, such as logging and gzip compression.

types.name: Logger
types.doc: Logger writes a one-line summary of every request to Out.

methods.name: Handler
methods.doc: Handler returns the middleware handler for this Logger.

funcs.name: Gzip
funcs.doc:
Gzip returns middleware that compresses responses at the given level.
Use one of the Level constants.
